revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '麻績天長白羽神(ながしらはねのみこと)は[[天白信仰]]の神。伊勢忌部氏、伊勢国麻続の祖。 機織りの神とされ、伊勢神…'
revision.text:
麻績天長白羽神(ながしらはねのみこと)は[[天白信仰]]の神。伊勢忌部氏、伊勢国麻続の祖。
機織りの神とされ、伊勢神宮の御師により全国に広まったとされる。
==別名== *長白羽神 - [[古語拾遺]] 伊勢国麻続祖 *天の白羽神 - [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[菰野町]] *天白羽鳥神 - 阿波忌部氏系図 伊勢国麻続祖
==同一神とされるもの== *[[多比理伎志摩流美神]] - [[多比鹿神社]]（[[三重県]][[度会郡]][[菰野町]]）
== 関連 == *[[伊勢神宮]] *[[天白社]]
{{stub}}

麻績天長白羽神(ながしらはねのみこと)は[[天白信仰]]の神。伊勢忌部氏、伊勢国麻続の祖。
機織りの神とされ、伊勢神宮の御師により全国に広まったとされる。
==別名== *長白羽神 - [[古語拾遺]] 伊勢国麻続祖 [[天鷲翔矢命]]の子 *天の白羽神 - [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[菰野町]] *天白羽鳥神 - 阿波忌部氏系図 伊勢国麻続祖

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | name = Nagashiraha no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity_type = [[Amatsukami]] | full_name = Nagashiraha no Kami | aliases = Nagashiraha no Mikoto, Amashiraha no Torinomikoto, Amashiraha no Kami, Ame no Shiraha no Kami, etc. | alternative_titles = | divine_rank = None | divine_roles = [[Weaving]] | father = [[Amewakahayahi no Mikoto]] | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | siblings = | children = | shrines = [[Ame no Shiraha Shrine]] and others | blessings = | texts = | related_clans = Kamomibe (神麻績連) }}
'''Nagashiraha no Kami''' (長白羽神) is a deity featured in the *{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}*, appearing in the story of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). The deity was tasked by {{ill|Omoikane|ja|思兼神}} to grow flax and weave the blue sacred cloth known as "Aonigite." Nagashiraha no Kami is revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamomibe (神麻績部), who wove the "Aratama" (荒妙, hemp cloth) offered at {{ill|Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja|神麻続機殿神社}} for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame no Shiraha Shrine|ja|天志良波神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} shrine located in the former {{ill|Kujigun|ja|久慈郡}}, {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}} (now {{ill|Hitachiota|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | name = Nagashiraha no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity_type = [[Amatsukami]] | full_name = Nagashiraha no Kami | aliases = Nagashiraha no Mikoto, Amashiraha no Torinomikoto, Amashiraha no Kami, Ame no Shiraha no Kami, etc. | alternative_titles = | divine_rank = None | divine_roles = [[Weaving]] | father = [[Amewakahayahi no Mikoto]] | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | siblings = | children = | shrines = [[Ame no Shiraha Shrine]] and others | blessings = | texts = | related_clans = Kamomibe (神麻績連) }}
'''Nagashiraha no Kami''' (長白羽神) is a deity featured in the *{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}*, appearing in the story of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). The deity was tasked by {{ill|Omoikane|ja|思兼神}} to grow flax and weave the blue sacred cloth known as "Aonigite." Nagashiraha no Kami is revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamomibe (神麻績部), who wove the "Aratama" (荒妙, hemp cloth) offered at {{ill|Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja|神麻続機殿神社}} for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}.
The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame no Shiraha Shrine|ja|天志良波神社}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} shrine located in the former {{ill|Kujigun|ja|久慈郡}}, {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}} (now {{ill|Hitachiota|ja|常陸太田市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:長白羽神]][[zh:長白羽神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11653519]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity Japan | name = Nagashiraha no Kami | image = | image_size = | image_caption = | generation_name = | predecessor = | successor = | deity_type = [[Amatsukami]] | full_name = Nagashiraha no Kami | aliases = Nagashiraha no Mikoto, Amashiraha no Torinomikoto, Amashiraha no Kami, Ame no Shiraha no Kami, etc. | alternative_titles = | divine_Rank = None | divine_roles = [[Weaving]] | father = [[Amewakahayahi no Mikoto]] | mother = | relatives = | spouse = | siblings = | children = | shrines = [[Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine]] and others | blessings = | texts = | related_clans = Kamomibe (神麻績連) }}
'''Nagashiraha no Kami''' (長白羽神) is a deity featured in the *[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]*, appearing in the story of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). The deity was tasked by {{ill|Omoikane|ja|思兼神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoikane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ}} to grow flax and weave the blue sacred cloth known as "Aonigite." Nagashiraha no Kami is revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamomibe (神麻績部), who wove the "Aratama" (荒妙, hemp cloth) offered at {{ill|Hatadono Shrines|lt=Hatadono Shrines|lt=Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja|神麻続機殿神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社}} for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] shrine located in the former [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]], [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (now [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] shrine located in the former [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]], [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (now [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

'''Nagashiraha no Kami''' (長白羽神) is a deity featured in the *[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]*, appearing in the story of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). The deity was tasked by {{ill|Omoikane|ja|思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoikane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ}} to grow flax and weave the blue sacred cloth known as "Aonigite." Nagashiraha no Kami is revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamomibe (神麻績部), who wove the "Aratama" (荒妙, hemp cloth) offered at {{ill|Hatadono Shrines|lt=Hatadono Shrines|lt=Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja|神麻続機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社}} for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].

This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] shrine located in the former [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]], [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (now [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] shrine located in the former [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]], [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (now [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

'''Nagashiraha no Kami''' (長白羽神) is a deity featured in the *[[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]]*, appearing in the story of the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸}} (Heavenly Rock Cave). The deity was tasked by {{ill|Omoikane|ja|思兼神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omoikane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ}} to grow flax and weave the blue sacred cloth known as "Aonigite." Nagashiraha no Kami is revered as the ancestral deity of the Kamomibe (神麻績部), who wove the "Aratama" (荒妙, hemp cloth) offered at {{ill|Hatadono Shrines|lt=Hatadono Shrines|lt=Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja|神麻続機殿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmisokihata-dono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 機殿神社}} for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]].
The name "Shiraha" (白羽, "white feather") for white garments is said to derive from Nagashiraha no Kami. This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] shrine located in the former [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]], [[:en:Hitachi Province|Hitachi Province]] (now [[:en:Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>

This deity is considered a kinship god of {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|lt=Amenofutodama no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} but does not appear in the main {{ill|Kojiki|ja|記紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} myths. Also known as Ame no Shiraha no Kami (天白羽神), Nagashiraha no Kami is sometimes linked to the {{ill|Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku faith|lt=Tenpaku Faith|ja|天白信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpaku Faith|qq=}}.
The deity is enshrined as the principal god at {{ill|Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine|qq=}}, an {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} shrine located in the former {{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki|en|Kuji District, Ibaraki|WD=Q1075212|lt=Kujigun}}, {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province}} (now {{ill|Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|en|Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|WD=Q838663|lt=Hitachiota}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}).<ref>‘Shinto Encyclopedia’ (Rinsen Shoten)</ref>
